reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless Chrome real-inference verification (fresh + cached passes) + desktop/mobile screenshots + responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real headless run (WASM, no-GPU): fresh profile → Download (~155 MB) → real output. For 'The capital of France is [MASK].' ModernBERT returned Paris 88.8% (logit 20.25), Tours 2.5%, Madrid 1.4%, Rome 1.3% — a correct, well-separated distribution. Console errors: 0. Source calls AutoModelForMaskedLM in models/modernbert-fill-mask/worker.js (Web Worker); no canned path.
rubric.notes: Genuine answerdotai/ModernBERT-base ONNX via transformers.js 3.7.5 (ModernBertForMaskedLM class present in the shared 3.7.5 pin — no version pin needed).

rubric.evidence: fill-mask output is exactly the expected shape: ranked token candidates for the [MASK] position with softmax probabilities AND the raw pre-softmax logit for each (read from the [1, seq, 50368] logits tensor). Verified live: 8 ranked rows with token + % + logit; multi-mask supported.
rubric.notes: The 'see inside' surface reads real logits, not the pipeline's rounded scores — the probability bar and the exact logit both come from the same forward pass.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance (screenshot output-light.png) shows the exact source id answerdotai/ModernBERT-base linked to its card, task fill-mask, backend WebAssembly ('no WebGPU needed'), quantisation q8, context length 8192, download ~155 MB, license Apache-2.0. Inference off the main thread (worker.js).
rubric.notes: Honest, complete runtime disclosure; the ModernBERT-vs-BERT architecture table is factually accurate (22 layers, RoPE, GeGLU, alternating global/128-token-local attention, 8192 context, 50,368 BPE vocab — all from the real config.json).

rubric.evidence: Two-pass headless test on a persistent profile. PASS 1 (fresh): loader state 'download-required', an honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' + Download button — no silent ~155 MB transfer. PASS 2 (cached reload): loader state 'ready' immediately, NO Download button, real output rendered — auto-init from cache works exactly per the shared createModelLoader contract.
rubric.notes: shared-auto-init-loader = pass (createModelLoader). Per-model clear-cache control present.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live region announces checking→initialising→ready; a .err status surfaces prediction failures on the page. Verified the download-required and ready states live; retry path exists in the shared loader.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and forced-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven in this pass.

rubric.evidence: Real controls: a multi-line textarea (long text supported), five sample chips, an 'Insert [MASK] at cursor' button, a top-k range slider, and a live token/context meter. Verified they render and the debounced re-run fires on input. Chips are real <button> elements; the range has a visible value.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard traversal (focus order + visible focus on every control at both viewports) is the remaining check before flipping mobile to ok.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the output area is empty and the loader shows the honest absent-model state; predictions appear only after a real on-device run. Nothing canned is presented as live output.

buttons-have-names and images-have-alt hold (no content images beyond the SVG favicon). Status region uses role=status aria-live=polite; the prediction rows are keyboard-activatable buttons that fill the chosen token.
rubric.notes: Tables use th scopes; the arch table has a caption.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth<=innerWidth, zero clipped controls, console+network clean. Wide tables sit in .table-scroll (overflow-x:auto) so they never force page overflow. Desktop real-inference screenshots (light output-light.png + dark pass2-dark.png) confirm legible reflow.
rubric.notes: Desktop flipped to ok (full driven matrix pass); mobile kept needs-review pending a driven touch-interaction pass.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a Web Worker (off main thread); the input handler is debounced (350 ms) so long-context typing doesn't spike INP. Verified the page stays responsive during the ~155 MB load and inference.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while typing into a very long passage would confirm the long-context path stays smooth.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (flex: 1 1 <basis>, min-inline-size:0) and put wide tables in an overflow-x:auto container so nothing forces horizontal page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — control rows use flex-wrap; the At-a-glance and ModernBERT-vs-BERT tables sit in .table-scroll; verified no horizontal overflow at 360×740.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread and yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — masked-LM inference runs in models/modernbert-fill-mask/worker.js; the input handler is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: inference-off-main-thread source assertion; page stayed responsive during load

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; give every control an accessible name and keyboard operation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live for load/run state; labelled buttons/sliders; prediction rows are keyboard-activatable buttons.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css (invariant 6).

openQuestions: Drive a full touch-interaction pass at 360×740 (focus order, 44px tap targets on chips/slider) before flipping mobile support to ok.
Measure INP while typing into a multi-thousand-token passage — does the long-context forward pass ever block the main thread noticeably?
Should the demo offer a WebGPU path (q4f16) for faster long-context inference on capable devices, with an honest WASM fallback?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for modernbert-fill-mask: drive every control on mobile with touch, verify tap targets/focus, then flip mobile support to ok.
Add a 'long-context retrieval' wild demo that hides a fact thousands of tokens deep and visualises how far back the answer lived — making the 8192 context tangible.

summary: modernbert-fill-mask is a strong, honest demo of a genuinely new 2024 encoder: real fill-mask inference via answerdotai/ModernBERT-base ONNX (ModernBertForMaskedLM in transformers.js 3.7.5, no version pin) in a Web Worker, verified end-to-end in headless Chrome — fresh Download → correct output (Paris 88.8%), cached reload → auto-init with no button, console clean both passes. The page emphasises the architecture leap (RoPE, GeGLU, alternating attention, 8192 context) with an accurate at-a-glance table and a ModernBERT-vs-BERT comparison, and reads the exact masked-token logits for the 'see inside' surface. Desktop support flipped to ok on a full driven matrix pass; mobile kept needs-review (automated-clean layout + screenshot) pending a driven touch pass. Revision 1.
